directionBias: Keep Poword centered on a lightweight Windows-wide text-selection translation product.
Treat Rust as the product trunk and long-term implementation center of gravity.
Treat Java as short-term compatibility infrastructure unless repository truth requires otherwise.
techBias: Prefer Rust for new durable product surface area and Windows-native interaction loops.
Keep Java changes narrow, explicit, and migration-friendly.

docsSummary: Poword is being steered toward a Windows-native overlay flow for translation and vocabulary capture.
The active direction prioritizes lightweight desktop behavior over browser-only extension coupling.

projectConstraints: Keep all harness state outside the Poword repository.
Bias toward one coherent step of Windows-native product progress per harness cycle.
Do not expand the heavy desktop footprint when a smaller native path is available.
preferredChecks: Run Java compile or Maven validation when touching legacy Java paths.
Run Cargo-oriented checks when touching Rust migration paths.
environmentHotspots: Windows shell quoting and process launch behavior

directionBias: Keep the strategy focused on Windows-wide product fit and Rust-mainline migration.
Sequence milestones so the Windows overlay loop lands before wider product expansion.
Prefer the next smallest implementable Rust-leaning slice that advances the overlay and vocabulary loop.

techBias: When possible, move product logic toward Rust instead of deepening Java ownership.
projectConstraints: Keep code changes reviewable and migration-friendly across Java and Rust boundaries.

environmentHotspots: Local gateway availability and Windows sandbox execution behavior
Rust toolchain readiness, Maven command shape, and Windows-native process prerequisites

preferredChecks: Prefer evaluation evidence that confirms lightweight Windows-native behavior and migration-safe progress.
